title: Lampranthus furvus
taxonomy: Lampranthus furvus is classified within the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant or succulent family. This classification places it among other succulent plants, which are characterized by their water-storing leaves. The genus Lampranthus is further grouped within the tribe Ruschieae, a subset of the Aizoaceae family. The family Aizoaceae itself belongs to the order Caryophyllales, which includes various flowering plants with similar characteristics. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared traits among these plants.

intro: Lampranthus furvus, also known as the Firecracker Ice Plant, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and fleshy, green leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lampranthus furvus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Lampranthus furvus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory infections.
